caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) for IRAs and retirement plans. The page explains that traditional IRA, SEP, SARSEP, and SIMPLE IRA account holders must start taking distributions by age 72, even if they are still working. For those reaching age 72 in 2022, the first RMD is due by April 1, 2023, and subsequent RMDs by December 31 each year thereafter. It also covers RMD rules for 401(k), 403(b), and 457(b) plans, profit-sharing plans, and defined benefit plans, noting that the first RMD is due by April 1 of the later of the year they reach age 72 or when they are no longer employed. A 5% owner of the employer must begin RMDs at age 72. The video highlights that RMDs cannot be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan and mentions a comparison chart for basic RMD rules. Roth IRAs do not require distributions while the original owner is alive. The cursor moves slightly, indicating navigation through the text.
